current_goal: Implement thread-bundle handoff sync so a user can move between machines and resume Codex work from repo-related thread data.
status_summary: The local reader CLI and Cloudflare R2 remote auth profile flow are implemented. The next milestone is repo attach plus thread discovery, thread-bundle export, background push/pull sync, and root view materialization for another machine.
next_prompt: Implement the npm installer, repo registry, local thread discovery from the Codex thread list, thread-bundle export under `.codex-handoff/threads/`, and background sync that materializes the selected thread into the root handoff view.

decisions.summary: Always bootstrap from latest.md before searching raw history.
decisions.rationale: Keeps routine restore cheap and deterministic.

decisions.summary: Treat raw jsonl as evidence that must be searched and sliced on demand.
decisions.rationale: Prevents loading the whole session transcript into Codex.

decisions.summary: Start with a local CLI instead of an MCP server.
decisions.rationale: The CLI is simpler to ship and is enough to prove the restore model.

decisions.summary: Use `remote` as the user-facing term for synchronized backend storage.
decisions.rationale: It keeps the backend abstraction stable while R2 is the only concrete provider.

decisions.summary: Design the next installation layer around an npm wrapper and a background agent.
decisions.rationale: The target experience is a short Codex prompt that installs, authenticates, auto-starts, and syncs across macOS and Windows.

decisions.summary: Scope sync by repo-attached thread bundles, not by flattening all handoff files into one remote root.
decisions.rationale: The product is for one person handing work off between machines, so the remote layout should preserve thread identity while still materializing a single active view for the existing reader.

todos.summary: Implement status, resume, context-pack, search, and extract commands.
Define the handoff.json schema and keep a sample file in the repository.
Document the AGENTS bootstrap flow and CLI usage examples.
Write the agent-first installer UX, npm wrapper spec, ready-to-paste install prompts, and thread-bundle sync design.
Implement the npm installer, repo registry, local thread discovery, thread-bundle export, and background pull/push sync flow.

recent_commands.purpose: Confirm the baseline interpreter for the zero-dependency CLI.
Verify the reader CLI and R2 auth profile flow after implementation.

sessions.summary: Discussion about splitting latest.md, handoff.json, and raw jsonl roles.

notes: The reader is responsible for generating Codex-friendly context, not just copying raw files.
Resume quality depends on keeping latest.md concise and handoff.json structured.
The sync model should optimize for serial handoff between machines, not simultaneous editing.
Thread identity must be preserved in remote storage, but the existing reader should keep consuming a single root `.codex-handoff/` view.
